title: Add Numbers in Scientific Notation (Same Exponent)
description: Add coefficients when two numbers share the same power of 10 and keep the exponent unchanged, then normalize if needed.
condition: Two numbers written as a×10^n and b×10^n with the same exponent.
response: Compute (a+b)×10^n and rewrite in proper scientific notation if necessary.
label: Principle / Model

title: Align Exponents Before Adding/Subtracting
description: To add or subtract numbers in scientific notation, first rewrite them with the same power of 10.
condition: Expression of the form a × 10^m ± b × 10^n where m ≠ n.
response: Rewrite one number so both have the same exponent, then combine coefficients.
label: Principle / Model

title: Apply the Pythagorean Theorem
description: Find a missing side length in a right triangle using a^2+b^2=c^2.
condition: A right triangle with two known side lengths.
response: Substitute into a^2+b^2=c^2 and solve for the unknown side.
label: Principle / Model

title: Classify Fraction as Rational
description: Recognize that any ratio of integers with nonzero denominator is rational.
condition: Given a number expressed as a fraction of integers (a/b, b ≠ 0).
response: Classify the number as rational.
label: Concept

title: Classify Nonterminating Nonrepeating Decimal as Irrational
description: Recognize that a decimal that does not terminate and does not repeat is irrational.
condition: Given a decimal representation that is nonterminating and nonrepeating.
response: Classify the number as irrational.
label: Concept

title: Classify Square Root of Non‑Perfect Square as Irrational
description: Recognize that the square root of a non‑perfect square integer is irrational.
condition: Given √n where n is not a perfect square integer.
response: Classify √n as irrational.
label: Concept

title: Classify Square Root of Perfect Square as Rational
description: Recognize that the square root of a perfect square integer is rational.
condition: Given √n where n is a perfect square integer.
response: Evaluate the root and classify it as rational.
label: Principle / Model

title: Classify Terminating Decimal as Rational
description: Recognize that a decimal that terminates can be written as a fraction and is rational.
condition: Given a terminating decimal.
response: Classify the number as rational.
label: Concept

title: Combine Coefficients with Common Exponent
description: After aligning exponents, add or subtract the decimal coefficients and keep the common power of 10.
condition: Two scientific notation numbers with identical powers of 10 are being added or subtracted.
response: Add or subtract the coefficients and retain the shared exponent.
label: Principle / Model

title: Compare by Exponent Magnitude
description: When ordering numbers in scientific notation, compare powers of 10 first; larger exponents indicate larger magnitudes (for positive coefficients).
condition: Two or more positive numbers written in scientific notation.
response: Order numbers by exponent value first; if equal, compare coefficients.
label: Principle / Model

title: Compare Quantities in Scientific Notation by Ratio
description: Determine how many times larger one quantity is than another using division of coefficients and subtraction of exponents.
condition: Two quantities written in scientific notation a×10^m and b×10^n to be compared multiplicatively.
response: Compute (a/b)×10^(m−n) and interpret as a multiplicative comparison.
label: Principle / Model

title: Compute Ratio in Scientific Notation
description: To determine how many times one scientific notation number is of another, divide coefficients and subtract exponents.
condition: Given a × 10^m and b × 10^n, and asked how many times one is of the other.
response: Compute (a/b) × 10^(m−n) and simplify.
label: Principle / Model

title: Convert Miles to Inches
description: Convert a measurement in miles to inches using unit conversion factors.
condition: A length is given in miles and must be expressed in inches.
response: Multiply by 5280 feet per mile and 12 inches per foot.
label: Principle / Model

title: Convert Standard Form to Scientific Notation
description: Rewrite a decimal number as a value between 1 and 10 multiplied by a power of 10.
condition: A decimal number not written in scientific notation.
response: Move the decimal to create a number between 1 and 10 and multiply by the appropriate power of 10.
label: Principle / Model

title: Convert Units Using Multiplicative Factors
description: Convert a measurement to a more appropriate unit by multiplying by a known conversion factor.
condition: A quantity given in one unit and a known conversion factor to another unit.
response: Multiply by the conversion factor to express the quantity in the new unit.
label: Principle / Model

title: Divide Numbers in Scientific Notation
description: Divide coefficients and subtract exponents when dividing numbers written in scientific notation.
condition: A quotient (a×10^m) ÷ (b×10^n).
response: Compute (a/b)×10^(m−n) and normalize to scientific notation.
label: Principle / Model

title: Evaluate a Cube Root
description: Determine the number whose cube equals a given value.
condition: Given an expression of the form \sqrt[3]{n}.
response: Find the number x such that x^3 = n.
label: Principle / Model

title: Evaluate a Square Root
description: Determine the principal square root of a nonnegative number.
condition: Given an expression of the form \sqrt{n} with n \ge 0.
response: Find the nonnegative number x such that x^2 = n.
label: Principle / Model

title: Evaluate Expression with Exponent Rules
description: Apply power, product, and quotient rules of exponents to simplify expressions.
condition: Expression involving powers with the same base, such as (a^m)^n, a^m·a^n, or a^m/a^n.
response: Use exponent rules to combine exponents and simplify.
label: Principle / Model

title: Identify π as Irrational
description: Recall that π is an irrational number.
condition: Given the constant π.
response: Classify π as irrational.
label: Fact

title: Locate Square Root Between Consecutive Integers
description: Determine between which two whole numbers a non-perfect square root lies.
condition: Given a positive number n that is not a perfect square and asked to bound \sqrt{n} between consecutive integers.
response: Find consecutive integers k and k+1 such that k^2 < n < (k+1)^2, then conclude k < \sqrt{n} < k+1.
label: Principle / Model

title: Match Radical to Approximate Value
description: Estimate square or cube roots and match them to reasonable decimal approximations.
condition: A radical expression such as √n or ∛n with answer choices in decimal form.
response: Estimate the root using nearby perfect squares or cubes and select the closest value.
label: Production / Skill

title: Multiply Numbers in Scientific Notation
description: To multiply numbers in scientific notation, multiply coefficients and add exponents.
condition: Expression of the form (a × 10^m)(b × 10^n).
response: Compute (a × b) × 10^(m + n), then adjust to proper scientific notation if needed.
label: Principle / Model

title: Negative Exponent as Reciprocal
description: A negative exponent indicates the reciprocal of the base raised to the corresponding positive exponent.
condition: Expression of the form a^{-n} where a ≠ 0
response: Rewrite as 1/a^n
label: Principle / Model

title: Order Numbers by Magnitude
description: Order a mixture of standard and scientific notation numbers by comparing their magnitudes.
condition: A list of positive numbers in standard and/or scientific notation must be ordered from least to greatest.
response: Convert to comparable forms or compare powers of 10 and coefficients to arrange in ascending order.
label: Principle / Model

title: Order Real Numbers Using Approximations
description: Compare and order real numbers (including radicals and π) by converting to decimal approximations.
condition: Given a set of real numbers in different forms (e.g., radicals, π, integers).
response: Approximate each value and arrange them from least to greatest.
label: Principle / Model

title: Power of a Power Rule
description: When raising a power to a power, multiply the exponents.
condition: Expression of the form (a^m)^n
response: Rewrite as a^(m·n)
label: Principle / Model

title: Power of a Product Rule
description: When raising a product to a power, apply the exponent to each factor.
condition: Expression of the form (ab)^n
response: Rewrite as a^n b^n
label: Principle / Model

title: Product of Powers Rule
description: When multiplying powers with the same base, add the exponents.
condition: Expression of the form a^m · a^n with the same nonzero base
response: Rewrite as a^(m+n)
label: Principle / Model

title: Quotient of Powers Rule
description: When dividing powers with the same base, subtract the exponents.
condition: Expression of the form a^m / a^n with the same nonzero base
response: Rewrite as a^(m−n)
label: Principle / Model

title: Reason About Range of Hypotenuse Values
description: Determine possible values of the hypotenuse based on an interval for a leg.
condition: Right triangle with one leg fixed and the other constrained to an interval.
response: Evaluate the hypotenuse at interval endpoints and conclude all values lie between those results.
label: Principle / Model

title: Rewrite to Proper Scientific Notation
description: Ensure the coefficient in scientific notation is at least 1 and less than 10 by adjusting the exponent accordingly.
condition: A number is written as a × 10^n where a ≥ 10 or a < 1.
response: Adjust a to 1 ≤ a < 10 and change the exponent to keep the value equivalent.
label: Principle / Model

title: Round to Specified Decimal Place
description: Round a decimal number to a given place value using standard rounding rules.
condition: A decimal number and a specified place value (e.g., nearest ten‑thousandth or hundredth).
response: Check the next digit and round up or down accordingly.
label: Rule / Plan
example: $0.000298$ rounded to the nearest ten‑thousandth is $0.0003$.

title: Solve a Cubic Equation Using Cube Roots
description: Solve equations of the form x^3 = k by taking the cube root of both sides.
condition: Equation of the form x^3 = k.
response: Take the cube root of both sides to get x = \sqrt[3]{k}.
label: Principle / Model

title: Solve a Quadratic by Square Root Property
description: Solve equations of the form x^2 = k by taking both positive and negative square roots.
condition: Equation of the form x^2 = k where k > 0.
response: State x = \pm \sqrt{k}.
label: Principle / Model

title: Solve for Unknown Exponent
description: Determine an unknown exponent by rewriting both sides with a common base and equating exponents.
condition: Equation where both sides are powers of the same nonzero base, one with unknown exponent
response: Set exponents equal and solve for the variable
label: Principle / Model
notes: Depends on one-to-one property of exponential expressions

title: Subtract Numbers in Scientific Notation
description: Rewrite quantities with like exponents and subtract coefficients.
condition: Two quantities to subtract, possibly requiring rewriting one in scientific notation.
response: Express both with the same power of 10, subtract coefficients, and normalize.
label: Principle / Model

title: Use Ratio to Compare Magnitudes
description: Determine how many times larger one quantity is than another by dividing their values.
condition: Statement asking how many times as large one scientific-notation quantity is compared to another.
response: Compute the quotient of the two quantities and interpret the result as a multiplicative comparison.
label: Principle / Model

title: Zero Exponent Rule
description: Any nonzero number raised to the zero power equals 1.
condition: Expression of the form a^0 where a ≠ 0
response: Rewrite as 1
label: Concept
